description_long: This stone gatehouse features four prominent cylindrical corner towers, each topped with a conical blue-green roof and small windows. The main structure is constructed from large, gray stone blocks, giving it a robust and fortified appearance. The front facade is dominated by a large wooden double door set within a rectangular stone frame, flanked by symmetrical rows of rectangular windows. The battlements along the top edge of the walls and towers add to its defensive character, while the open courtyard within is visible from the rear. The base is surrounded by a grassy terrain, suggesting it is set within a landscaped area. The overall design evokes a medieval architectural style, emphasizing security and grandeur.
description: A fortified stone gatehouse with four conical-roofed towers, battlements, and a large wooden entry door, set on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main wooden double door, symmetrical windows, and two prominent front towers with blue-green conical roofs.
description_view_1: Angled rear-side view revealing the open courtyard, battlements, and the side profiles of the towers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back entrance, battlements, and the inner courtyard, with all four towers visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view highlighting the rear windows, battlements, and the side towers with their conical roofs.
attribute_entryway_design: Rectangular stone entry with large wooden double doors.
attribute_window_placement: Symmetrical rectangular windows flanking the entry and distributed along the walls and towers.
